sources.title: Are Options Traders Betting on a Big Move in TPG Stock?
sources.excerpt: Investors in **TPG Inc.** TPG need to pay close attention to the stock based on moves in the options market lately. That is because the Aug. 21, 2026 $37.50 Call had some of the highest implied volatility of all equity options today. ## What is Implied Volatility? Implied volatility shows how much movement the market is expecting in the future. Options with high levels of implied volatility suggest that investors in the underlying stocks are expecting a big move in one direction or the other. It could also mean there is an event coming up soon that may cause a big rally or a huge sell-off. However, implied volatility is only one piece of the puzzle when putting together an options trading strategy. ## What do the Analysts Think? Clearly, options traders are pricing in a big move for TPG shares, but what is the fundamental picture for the company? Currently, TPG is a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) in the Financial - Investment Management industry that ranks in the Bottom 19% of our Zacks Industry Rank. Over the last 60 days, no analyst increased the earnings estimates for the current quarter, while four have dropped their estimates. The net effect has taken our Zacks Consensus Estimate for the current quarter from 65 cents per share to 62 cents in that period. Given the way analysts feel about TPG right now, this huge implied volatility could mean there's a trade developing. Oftentimes, options traders look for options with high levels of implied volatility to sell premium. This is a strategy many seasoned traders use because it captures decay. At expiration, the hope for these traders is that the underlying stock does not move as much as originally expected. Want the latest recommendations from Zacks Investment Research? Today, you can download 7 Best Stocks for the Next 30 Days.

sources.title: TPG Appoints Axel André as Chief Financial Officer
sources.excerpt: **SAN FRANCISCO & FORT WORTH, Texas, June 22, 2026**\--([BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com))--TPG Inc. (NASDAQ: TPG), a leading global alternative asset management firm, today announced that Axel André has been named Partner and Chief Financial Officer of TPG, effective July 27, 2026. Mr. André will succeed Jack Weingart, who will transition fully into his position as the CEO of TPG's Global Wealth Solutions business, a role he added to his responsibilities last year focused on leading and building out the firm's wealth platform. "Over the past five years, TPG has transformed into a scaled and diversified platform shaping the future of alternative investing," said Jon Winkelried, Chief Executive Officer of TPG. "Axel brings a unique set of skills, which we view as highly complementary to our expanding set of capabilities, and the strategic vision to continue accelerating our growth strategy. He is stepping into this role with a strong foundation beneath him at a time of significant momentum for the firm, and we are pleased to welcome him to the firm." Mr. André brings decades of experience driving the financial strategy of both public corporations and investment management organizations. He joins TPG from Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated (NYSE: RGA), where he served as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, and was a member of the Executive Committee. Prior to RGA, Mr. André was Chief Financial Officer of American Equity Life and Jackson National, and earlier served as Chief Financial Officer and Chief Risk Officer of the Individual and Group Retirement Services division of AIG. His career began at Goldman Sachs, where he ultimately served as Managing Director of Global Insurance Strategies for the Investment Management Division. "In a

analysis:
- **External commentary:** Options market data shows elevated implied volatility on the Aug. 21, 2026 $37.50 call, signaling traders expect a significant move [1]. However, analyst sentiment is cautious: Zacks ranks TPG a Hold (bottom 19% of industry), and over the past 60 days four analysts cut Q2 estimates while none raised them, dropping the consensus from $0.65 to $0.62 [1]. Separately, TPG appointed Axel André as CFO effective July 27, 2026, replacing Jack Weingart who moves to lead Global Wealth Solutions [2][3]. The CEO cited “significant momentum” and a “strong foundation” at the firm [3].

- **Why it can work:** The CFO transition may signal strategic focus on scaling the wealth platform, potentially unlocking new revenue streams [3]. The stock trades 43% below its 52-week high with a forward P/E of 11.58, which could attract value-oriented investors if earnings stabilize. The RSI acceleration from -6.4 to +14.1 (delta of 20.4) suggests recent selling pressure may be exhausting, and short interest at 6.1% of float could fuel a squeeze on positive news.

- **What can break it:** The trailing P/E of 179.13 indicates depressed earnings, and the 1-month return of -3.8% lags the sector median of +7.3% by 11.1 percentage points, showing persistent weakness. Analyst estimate cuts [1] imply fundamental headwinds are not yet priced in. The elevated options activity could simply reflect hedging or premium-selling strategies, not bullish conviction [1]. The CFO change introduces execution risk during a transition period.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for a reversal in analyst estimate revisions (e.g., at least one upgrade or positive guidance). Monitor the stock’s ability to reclaim its 50-day moving average and sustain above $40. Any insider buying or increase in institutional ownership would support the long thesis. A catalyst would be the new CFO’s strategic update or Q2 earnings that beat the lowered consensus.

- **Bottom line:** TPG shows technical signs of a potential reversal (RSI acceleration, deep discount from highs) and a possible strategic catalyst in the CFO change, but fundamental deterioration (falling estimates, sector underperformance) and cautious analyst sentiment [1] create significant risk. The long setup is speculative and depends on a fundamental turnaround that is not yet confirmed by the available data.

sources.title: Pfizer Inc. (PFE) Is a Trending Stock: Facts to Know Before Betting on It
sources.excerpt: **Pfizer** (PFE) is one of the stocks most watched by Zacks.com visitors lately. So, it might be a good idea to review some of the factors that might affect the near-term performance of the stock. Over the past month, shares of this drugmaker have returned -9.5%, compared to the Zacks S&P 500 composite's -1.4% change. During this period, the Zacks Large Cap Pharmaceuticals industry, which Pfizer falls in, has gained 4.7%. The key question now is: What could be the stock's future direction? While media releases or rumors about a substantial change in a company's business prospects usually make its stock 'trending' and lead to an immediate price change, there are always some fundamental facts that eventually dominate the buy-and-hold decision-making. ## Revisions to Earnings Estimates Rather than focusing on anything else, we at Zacks prioritize evaluating the change in a company's earnings projection. This is because we believe the fair value for its stock is determined by the present value of its future stream of earnings. Our analysis is essentially based on how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to take the latest business trends into account. When earnings estimates for a company go up, the fair value for its stock goes up as well. And when a stock's fair value is higher than its current market price, investors tend to buy the stock, resulting in its price moving upward. Because of this, empirical studies indicate a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and short-term stock price movements. Pfizer is expected to post earnings of $0.68 per share for the current quarter, representing a year-over-year change of -12.8%. Over the last 30 days, the Zacks Consensus Estimate remained unchanged. sources.title: Pfizer Declares Third-Quarter 2026 Dividend - BioSpace
sources.excerpt: **_Board of Directors approves quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per share_** NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Pfizer Inc. (NYSE: PFE) today announced that its board of directors declared a $0.43 third-quarter 2026 dividend on the company’s common stock, payable September 1, 2026, to holders of the Common Stock of record at the close of business on July 24, 2026. [![](https://mms.businesswire.com/media/20260624889362/en/1045874/22/Pfizer_Logo_Color_RGB_APPROVED.jpg)](https://mms.businesswire.com/media/20260624889362/en/1045874/5/Pfizer_Logo_Color_RGB_APPROVED.jpg)[![](https://mms.businesswire.com/media/20260624889362/en/1045874/21/Pfizer_Logo_Color_RGB_APPROVED.jpg)](https://mms.businesswire.com/media/20260624889362/en/1045874/5/Pfizer_Logo_Color_RGB_APPROVED.jpg) Pfizer is committed to maintaining, and over the longer term, growing the dividend, as part of its capital allocation strategy. The third-quarter 2026 cash dividend will be the 351st consecutive quarterly dividend paid by Pfizer. _**About Pfizer: Breakthroughs That Change Patients’ Lives **_ At Pfizer, we apply science and our global resources to bring therapies to people that extend and significantly improve their lives. We strive to set the standard for quality, safety and value in the discovery, development and manufacture of health care products, including innovative medicines and vaccines. Every day, Pfizer colleagues work across developed and emerging markets to advance wellness, prevention, treatments and cures that challenge the most feared diseases of our time. Consistent with our responsibility as one of the world's premier innovative biopharmaceutical companies, we collaborate with health care providers, governments and local communities to support and expand access to reliable, affordable health care arou

analysis:
- **External commentary:** Source [1] notes Pfizer’s stock has returned -9.5% over the past month versus the S&P 500’s -1.4%, while its industry gained 4.7%, indicating significant underperformance. The same source emphasizes that earnings estimate revisions are the primary driver of fair value and short-term price moves, and reports that the Zacks Consensus Estimate for the current quarter remained unchanged over the last 30 days. Source [2] confirms a $0.43 quarterly dividend and a stated commitment to maintaining and growing it over the longer term. Source [3] announces a Q2 2026 earnings call on August 4, 2026, but provides no preliminary results or guidance.

- **Why it can work:** The stock is trading near its 52-week low (only 5% above it) with a forward P/E of 9.03, suggesting a deep value entry if earnings stabilize. The RSI acceleration score of 99.4 and a large positive RSI delta (18.8) after a negative prior average (-3.2) indicate a sharp short-term momentum shift, which could precede a mean-reversion rally. The dividend yield (implied ~7.1% at current price) provides a tangible floor for patient holders, and the company’s long history of consecutive dividends (351st quarter) signals financial stability.

- **What can break it:** Earnings estimates have not been revised upward (source [1] shows no change in 30 days), meaning the fundamental catalyst for a sustained re-rating is absent. The trailing P/E of 19.56 versus forward P/E of 9.03 implies expected earnings declines, and the PEG ratio of 13.77 suggests no growth premium. The stock has underperformed its sector by over 14 percentage points in the past month, and the sentiment score is low (0.4). If the August 4 earnings call (source [3]) reveals further guidance cuts or weak pipeline updates, the value trap could deepen.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Upward revisions to earnings estimates from sell-side analysts (source [1] framework) would be the strongest fundamental confirmation. Also watch for a sustained break above the 52-week high of -15.6% (i.e., above ~$28.80) on volume, and any positive commentary from the August 4 conference call regarding pipeline progress or cost savings. A dividend increase or special dividend announcement (source [2]) would also signal management confidence.

- **Bottom line:** The setup is a high-risk, deep-value mean-reversion play supported by extreme RSI acceleration and a low forward P/E, but it lacks fundamental catalyst from earnings estimate revisions. The dividend provides a backstop, but the stock’s severe sector underperformance and flat estimate trend suggest the market is pricing in structural headwinds. Without upward estimate revisions or positive news from the upcoming earnings call, the long thesis remains speculative.

sources.title: Sila Realty Trust Shareholders Approve Blue Owl Acquisition
sources.excerpt: Sila Realty Trust (SILA) said Friday its stockholders approved the company's acquisition by affiliat Upgrade to read this MT Newswires article and get so much more. A Silver or Gold subscription plan is required to access premium news articles.

sources.title: Sila Realty Trust, Inc. Stockholders Approve Acquisition by Affiliates of Blue Owl Capital Inc.
sources.excerpt: **TAMPA, Fla., June 26, 2026** \--([BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com))--Sila Realty Trust, Inc. (NYSE: SILA) ("Sila" or "the Company") today announced that, at a Special Meeting of Stockholders held earlier today, Sila stockholders voted to approve the acquisition of Sila by affiliates of Blue Owl Capital Inc. (NYSE: OWL) ("Blue Owl"). According to the results of the Special Meeting of Stockholders, more than 98% of votes cast at the meeting – which represented approximately 63% of the outstanding shares of Sila common stock as of the record date – were voted in favor of approving the merger agreement and the transactions contemplated thereby, including the merger. The final voting results of the Special Meeting of Stockholders will be filed as part of a Form 8-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") after certification by Sila's inspector of election. Under the terms of the merger agreement announced on April 20, 2026, common stockholders of the Company will receive $30.38 in cash for each share of Sila common stock owned. Subject to the satisfaction or waiver of all of the conditions to the closing of the transaction in the merger agreement, the transaction is expected to be completed on July 1, 2026. Upon closing of the transaction, Sila's common stock will no longer be listed on any public market. **About Sila Realty Trust, Inc.** Sila Realty Trust, Inc., headquartered in Tampa, Florida, is a net lease real estate investment trust with a strategic focus on investing in the growing and resilient healthcare sector. The Company invests in high quality healthcare facilities along the continuum of care in the pursuit of generating predictable, durable, and growing income streams. Sila's portfolio comprises high quality tenants in geograp

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Analysts have trimmed Blue Owl Capital’s fair value target from $14.50 to $13.31, citing lower interest yields, softer capital deployment, and credit pressure across the BDC sector [1]. Truist maintains a Buy rating, indicating some institutional support despite the downward revision [1]. Separately, Blue Owl’s affiliate acquisition of Sila Realty Trust received over 98% stockholder approval, expected to close July 1, 2026, adding a net-lease healthcare real estate portfolio [3]. The impact of this acquisition on OWL’s earnings or balance sheet is not detailed in the provided excerpts.

- **Why it can work**: The stock trades at a forward P/E of 9.58 and a PEG of 0.14, suggesting low valuation relative to expected growth. The RSI has accelerated sharply (delta +9.7, acceleration +11.7) from oversold levels, and short interest is elevated at 20.6% of float, creating potential for a squeeze if sentiment shifts. The Sila acquisition diversifies OWL’s asset base into healthcare net-lease real estate, which may provide stable cash flows and reduce reliance on BDC credit markets [3].

- **What can break it**: The primary risk is continued credit pressure and yield compression in the BDC space, which drove the fair value cut [1]. The stock is down 14.6% in the past month while the sector median gained 7.3%, indicating severe underperformance that may reflect fundamental headwinds. The trailing P/E of 78.5 is distorted by low earnings, and the sentiment score is negative (-0.6). The Sila acquisition’s financing terms and integration risks are unknown from the provided data.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor whether OWL’s price can break above its recent downtrend and hold above the 52-week low (currently only 8.8% above it). Watch for analyst upgrades or stabilization of fair value estimates. Track the Sila acquisition closing and any subsequent earnings guidance that quantifies accretion. A reduction in short interest or a positive shift in the sentiment score would support the setup.

- **Bottom line**: OWL presents a contrarian long setup with low valuation, high short interest, and a recent RSI acceleration, but the fundamental backdrop is challenged by sector-wide credit and yield pressures. The Sila acquisition adds a new asset class but its financial impact is unquantified. The setup is speculative and depends on a catalyst (e.g., acquisition closing, sector turnaround) to reverse the severe one-month peer lag. No trade recommendation is made.

sources.title: Morgan Stanley Cuts Price Target on Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH). Here is Why
sources.excerpt: With an annual dividend yield of 3.37%, Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:[CTSH](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CTSH)) is included among the [**12 Best NASDAQ Stocks to Buy for Dividends**](https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/12-best-nasdaq-stocks-to-buy- for-dividends-1789460/). Morgan Stanley Cuts Price Target on Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH). Here is Why Photo by [Annie Spratt](https://unsplash.com/@anniespratt?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=unsplash&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=creditCopyText) Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) is an AI Builder and technology services provider, bridging the gap between AI investment and enterprise value. On June 23, Morgan Stanley cut its price target on Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (NASDAQ:CTSH) from $63 to $44, but reaffirmed its 'Equal Weight' rating on the shares. The lowered target still implies an upside of almost 4% from the current price level. Morgan Stanley believes that demand trends are "stable-to-slightly worse", especially when it comes to securing larger managed services deals. According to the firm, the lack of any demand acceleration through mid-June raises concerns about the market's expectations for a strong second half of the year, creating potential downside risks to the current Wall Street forecasts. [**Pzena I

analysis:
- **External commentary**: Morgan Stanley cut its price target from $63 to $44 (still ~4% upside) and noted demand trends are “stable-to-slightly worse,” especially for larger managed services deals, with no demand acceleration through mid-June [1]. Wedbush upgraded CTSH from Neutral to Outperform on June 8, raising its price objective by $14 [1]. The stock is also listed among “12 Best NASDAQ Stocks to Buy for Dividends” with a 3.37% yield [1]. No other analyst commentary was provided.

- **Why it can work**: The stock is 53.8% below its 52-week high, trades at a forward P/E of 8.59 and a PEG of 0.87, suggesting deep value territory if earnings stabilize. The RSI of 31.8 is oversold and has accelerated by 6.5 points, while short interest is elevated at 12.7% of float, creating potential for a short squeeze. The 1-month peer lag of 18.9% implies mean-reversion opportunity if sector sentiment improves.

- **What can break it**: Morgan Stanley’s view that demand is “stable-to-slightly worse” and that lack of acceleration through mid-June creates downside risk to H2 forecasts [1]. The stock has already fallen 26.3% in one month, and the sentiment score is very low at 0.2. If macro or IT services spending weakens further, the low forward P/E may not be a floor. The Wedbush upgrade is a positive but isolated call; broader analyst consensus is unknown from supplied data.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Any signs of demand acceleration (e.g., large deal wins, management guidance upgrades, or positive industry IT services spending data). A sustained RSI move above 40 and declining short interest would support a squeeze. Also watch for additional analyst upgrades or price target increases beyond Wedbush.

- **Bottom line**: CTSH is a deeply oversold, value-priced IT services stock with high short interest and a recent analyst upgrade, but the primary risk is that demand trends remain weak or worsen, as flagged by Morgan Stanley. The long setup depends on a catalyst for demand re-acceleration, which is not confirmed by the supplied excerpts. No trade recommendation.

analysis:
- **External commentary:** [1] notes CarMax beat Q2 expectations but management warned of operational inefficiencies and cost pressures. The company is focusing on refining retail pricing, vehicle selection, logistics, and digital customer experience. The stock has risen 30.2% in the past month but is down 20.7% over one year, indicating a recent rally against a weak longer-term trend.

- **Why it can work:** The recent price surge (+25.9% in one month vs. sector median +0.9%) and low forward P/E (17.13) relative to trailing P/E (28.27) suggest earnings growth expectations are improving. The PEG ratio of 0.49 implies undervaluation relative to growth. High short interest (10.7% of float) could fuel a squeeze if positive catalysts emerge. Management’s operational fixes, if effective, may improve margins and support a sustained turnaround.

- **What can break it:** [1] explicitly cites cost pressures and execution risks. The company’s own admission of inefficiencies means the turnaround is unproven. The stock is 26.1% below its 52-week high, and the composite value score (62.3) is moderate, not compelling. The sentiment score is negative (-0.4), and the RSI (57.3) is neutral, not confirming strong momentum. If operational improvements fail or consumer demand weakens, the recent rally could reverse.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for subsequent quarters showing improved operating margins and same-store sales growth. Monitor management’s progress on retail pricing and logistics changes as cited in [1]. A decline in short interest or an increase in institutional buying would support the setup. Positive earnings revisions from analysts would also be a confirming signal.

- **Bottom line:** The long setup is speculative. The stock has strong recent price momentum and a low PEG, but the company itself warns of cost and execution challenges. Without clear evidence of operational improvement from future reports, the rally may be unsustainable. No trade recommendation.

sources.title: Should You Buy Ralliant (RAL) After Golden Cross?
sources.excerpt: From a technical perspective, Ralliant Corporation (RAL) is looking like an interesting pick, as it just reached a key level of support. RAL's 50-day simple moving average crossed above its 200-day simple moving average, which is known as a "golden cross" in the trading world. Considered an important signifier for a bullish breakout, a golden cross is a technical chart pattern that's formed when a stock's short-term moving average breaks above a longer-term moving average; the most common crossover involves the 50-day and the 200-day, since bigger time periods tend to form stronger breakouts. There are three stages to a golden cross. First, there must be a downtrend in a stock's price that eventually bottoms out. Then, the stock's shorter moving average crosses over its longer moving average, triggering a positive trend reversal. The third stage is when a stock continues the upward momentum to higher prices. A golden cross contrasts with a death cross, another widely-followed chart pattern that suggests bearish momentum could be on the horizon. RAL could be on the verge of a breakout after moving 31.9% higher over the last four weeks. Plus, the company is currently a #2 (Buy) on the Zacks Rank. The bullish case solidifies once investors consider RAL's positive earnings outlook. For the current quarter, no earnings estimate has been cut compared to 5 revisions higher in the past 60 days. The Zacks Consensus Estimate has increased too. Moving Average Chart for RAL Given this move in earnings estimates and the positive technical factor, investors may want to keep their eye on RAL for more gains in the near future. [2] notes a recent share price of US$25.05, a 1.42% one-month decline, but a 16.89% three-month gain and a 17.11% year-to-date return, and claims a 20.5% undervaluation relative to an indicated intrinsic value. The stock has outperformed its Retail-Wholesale sector year-to-date (27.7% vs. -0.9% per [1]; 24.1% vs. 4.2% per [3]), indicating relative strength. The RSI of 45.6 is near neutral, not overbought, leaving room for upside if momentum re-accelerates. The forward P/E of 17.59 is lower than the trailing P/E of 36.3, implying expected earnings growth.

- **What can break it**: The RSI acceleration score of -6.6 and RSI delta of -6.4 indicate recent negative momentum, which could precede further downside if not reversed. The short interest is very low at 1.4%, meaning little bearish pressure to fuel a squeeze. The valuation claim of 20.5% undervaluation in [2] is unsupported by a disclosed model or peer comparison; the price-to-book ratio of 11.88 is high, suggesting potential overvaluation if earnings growth disappoints. The PEG ratio is null, so growth-adjusted valuation is unknown. Any reversal in the earnings estimate revision trend could break the setup.

- **Confirming evidence to watch**: Monitor next earnings report for actual EPS vs. consensus and any further upward estimate revisions. Watch for RSI to stabilize above 40 and turn upward, confirming momentum pullback completion. Track sector-relative performance: if ALH continues to outperform the Retail-Wholesale group, it supports the long thesis. A sustained price move above the recent 52-week high (implied by -5.5% from high) would signal strength.

- **Bottom line**: ALH shows a favorable earnings revision trend and sector outperformance, but recent negative price momentum and a high price-to-book ratio introduce risk. The claimed undervaluation is not independently verifiable from the supplied sources. The setup is plausible for a momentum pullback strategy only if earnings revisions continue and RSI recovers. No trade recommendation.

analysis:

The Helios data-center campus has begun generating revenue, adding a fresh income stream [1]. Recent stock repurchases signal management’s confidence in current positioning and future plans [1]. The stock is down 14.4% over the past week and 1.1% over the past month, yet up 18.2% year-to-date, framing the pullback as a potential entry point for those tracking the narrative [1].

- **Why it can work:** The start of Helios revenue provides a tangible, non-crypto income stream that may reduce the discount applied to Galaxy’s complex asset mix. Active buybacks at current levels suggest management sees intrinsic value above $29.26. The RSI acceleration score of 85.9 and a 12.1-point RSI delta over one period indicate a sharp reversal from prior negative momentum (prior 3-bar avg delta -2.8). Short interest at 12.5% of float could amplify upside if positive catalysts trigger a squeeze.

- **What can break it:** Forward P/E of 326x implies extreme earnings expectations; any miss on Helios revenue ramp or crypto market weakness could crush sentiment. The stock is 36% below its 52-week high, and the 1-month return of -8.9% underperforms the sector median (+7.3%) and a peer lag of +16.2%, suggesting company-specific headwinds. The composite value score of 35.5 is low, indicating the market may not agree with management’s undervaluation claim. Unknown: Helios revenue scale, profitability timeline, and whether buybacks will continue.

- **Confirming evidence to watch:** Look for next quarterly disclosure of Helios revenue and operating margin; an increase in buyback pace or authorization; sustained RSI above 50 with rising price; short interest declining (indicating squeeze exhaustion) or increasing (indicating further bearish conviction). Also monitor Bitcoin price trends, as Galaxy’s crypto exposure makes it highly correlated.

- **Bottom line:** The setup relies on management’s narrative of undervaluation and a new revenue catalyst (Helios) to reverse recent underperformance. High RSI acceleration and short interest offer tactical potential, but extreme valuation multiples and poor recent relative strength demand skepticism. No trade recommendation; further evidence of Helios profitability and sustained buybacks is needed to confirm the thesis.

sources.title: Las Vegas and Philadelphia Lead U.S. Hotel Growth in May 2026 - Hotel News Resource
sources.excerpt: **In Brief:** U.S. hotels saw year-over-year increases in occupancy, average daily rate and revenue per available room in May 2026, with Las Vegas and Philadelphia reporting the strongest market gains. * ![Philadelphia, PA, USA](https://www.hotelnewsresource.com/ul/4/2026/06/24/1782315835.webp) Among the Top 25 Markets, Philadelphia reported the highest occupancy increase, up 4.3% to 74.4%. - Image Credit Unsplash U.S. hotel performance showed positive year-over-year comparisons in May 2026, according to [CoStar](https://www.costargroup.com/ "CoStar") data. Nationwide occupancy reached 65.7%, up 0.6% from April 2025. The average daily rate (ADR) increased 3.4% to $168.51, while revenue per available room (RevPAR) rose 4% to $110.76. Among the Top 25 Markets, Philadelphia reported the highest occupancy increase, up 4.3% to 74.4%. Las Vegas recorded the largest gains in ADR, rising 13.5% to $238.40, and in RevPAR, up 17.9% to $188.69, supported by a strong calendar of events. Overall, 20 of the Top 25 Markets saw an increase in RevPAR.

Las Vegas recorded the largest gains in ADR, rising 13.5% to $238.40, and in RevPAR, up 17.9% to $188.69, supported by a strong calendar of events. Overall, 20 of the Top 25 Markets saw an increase in RevPAR.
